{{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|かかみのみやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]

{{神社 |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a {{ill|matsusha|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, it sits at the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by {{ill|Tanigawa|ja|谷川}} (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン}}.
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々―神社と聖地 第六巻 伊勢・志摩・伊賀・紀伊』[[白水社]]、1986年3月31日、492p. ISBN 4-560-02216-X

== 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area09/area09_16.jpg 鏡宮神社] }} {{commonscat|Kagami-no-miya-jinja}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0302.html 鏡宮神社（かがみのみやじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会
{{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|かかみのみやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kagamimiya Shrine-qq]] [[ja:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kagamimiya Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]

{{神社 |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a {{ill|matsusha|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, it sits at the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by {{ill|Tanigawa|ja|谷川}} (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン}}.

== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン}}.

{{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|かかみのみやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kagamimiya Shrine-qq]] [[ja:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kagamimiya Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]]

{{神社 |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a {{ill|matsusha|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, it sits at the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by {{ill|Tanigawa|ja|谷川}} (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>

; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン}}.

To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン}}.

{{神社 |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a {{ill|matsusha|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, it sits at the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by {{ill|Tanigawa|ja|谷川}} (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>

{{神社 |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a {{ill|matsusha|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, it sits at the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by {{ill|Tanigawa|ja|谷川}} (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン}}.

== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン}}.

{{神社 |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a {{ill|matsusha|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, it sits at the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by {{ill|Tanigawa|ja|谷川}} (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>

{{神社 |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a {{ill|matsusha|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, it sits at the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by {{ill|Tanigawa|ja|谷川}} (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン}}.

{{神社 |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a {{ill|matsusha|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, it sits at the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by {{ill|Tanigawa|ja|谷川}} (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン}}.

Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, it sits at the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by {{ill|Tanigawa|ja|谷川}} (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン}}.

{{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise|かかみのみやしんしや}} [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kagamimiya Shrine-qq]] [[ja:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:File]][[Category:File]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kagamimiya Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Kagamimiya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kagamimiya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kagamimiya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kagamimiya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kagamimiya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kagamimiya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kagamimiya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kagamimiya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kagamimiya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kagamimiya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kagamimiya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kagamimiya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kagamimiya Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kagamimiya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[ja:Kagamimiya Shrine]]

{{神社 |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a {{ill|matsusha|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, it sits at the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by {{ill|Tanigawa|ja|谷川}} (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン}}.

The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン}}.

{{神社 |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a {{ill|matsusha|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, it sits at the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by {{ill|Tanigawa|ja|谷川}} (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] to [[Category:Completion tier 3]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a {{ill|matsusha|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, it sits at the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by {{ill|Tanigawa|ja|谷川}} (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン}}.
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年3月5日、1690p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々―神社と聖地 第六巻 伊勢・志摩・伊賀・紀伊』[[白水社]]、1986年3月31日、492p. ISBN 4-560-02216-X

== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン}}.

{{神社 |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a {{ill|matsusha|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}) (Inner Shrine). {{神社 |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a {{ill|matsusha|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, it sits at the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by {{ill|Tanigawa|ja|谷川}} (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン}}.

Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, it sits at the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川}} and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by {{ill|Tanigawa|ja|谷川}} (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|近鉄鳥羽線}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン}}.
