revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a {{ill|matsusha|ja|摂末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsusha|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsusha|qq=}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City Office|qq=}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-chō, Ise|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, it sits at the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu River|qq=}} and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama River|qq=}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by {{ill|Tanigawa|ja|谷川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa|qq=}} (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|qq=}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred mirror|qq=}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yorishiro|qq=}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yorishiro|qq=}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|qq=}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Station|qq=}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzugawa Station|qq=}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzugaoka Station|qq=}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama IC|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Line|qq=}}.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々―神社と聖地 第六巻 伊勢・志摩・伊賀・紀伊』[[白水社]]、1986th year 3月31日、492p. ISBN 4-560-02216-X

{{神社 |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a {{ill|matsusha|ja|摂末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsusha|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} ({{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsusha|qq=}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City Office|qq=}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-chō, Ise|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, it sits at the confluence of the {{ill|Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu River|qq=}} and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama River|qq=}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by {{ill|Tanigawa|ja|谷川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanigawa|qq=}} (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|qq=}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the {{ill|sacred mirror|ja|神鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred mirror|qq=}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yorishiro|qq=}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Sengen shrine|lt=Sengen shrine|lt=Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a {{ill|yorishiro|ja|依り代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yorishiro|qq=}}, with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}, transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|qq=}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from {{ill|Asama Station|ja|朝熊駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Station|qq=}}. ** From {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzugawa Station|qq=}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from {{ill|Isuzugaoka Station|ja|五十鈴ケ丘駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzugaoka Station|qq=}} on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama IC|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Line|qq=}}.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々―神社と聖地 第六巻 伊勢・志摩・伊賀・紀伊』[[白水社]]、1986th year 3月31日、492p. ISBN 4-560-02216-X

It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsusha|qq=}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City Office|qq=}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-chō, Ise|qq=}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], it sits at the confluence of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama River|qq=}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by [[:en:Tanigawa|Tanigawa]] (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|qq=}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|sacred mirror]].<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]].<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Sengen shrine|lt=Sengen shrine|lt=Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]], with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|qq=}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from [[:en:Asama Station|Asama Station]]. ** From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the JR Sangu Line.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kagami-no-miya-jinja]][[ja:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17226516]] Wikidata name: Kagami-no-miya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17226516]] Wikidata name: Kagami-no-miya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.48722|N|136.75222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|matsusha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ([[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-chō, Ise|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], it sits at the confluence of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by [[:en:Tanigawa|Tanigawa]] (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|qq=}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|sacred mirror]].<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]].<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Sengen shrine|lt=Sengen shrine|lt=Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]], with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505724}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from [[:en:Asama Station|Asama Station]]. ** From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama IC|qq=|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Line|qq=|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}.

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 下巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 3月5日、1690p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々―神社と聖地 第六巻 伊勢・志摩・伊賀・紀伊』[[白水社]]、1986th year 3月31日、492p. ISBN 4-560-02216-X

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kagami-no-miya-jinja]][[ja:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17226516]] Wikidata name: Kagami-no-miya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17226516]] Wikidata name: Kagami-no-miya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.48722|N|136.75222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|matsusha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ([[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsusha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-chō, Ise|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], it sits at the confluence of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by [[:en:Tanigawa|Tanigawa]] (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|qq=}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|sacred mirror]].<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]].<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Sengen shrine|lt=Sengen shrine|lt=Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]], with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from [[:en:Asama Station|Asama Station]]. ** From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama IC|qq=|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Line|qq=|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kagami-no-miya-jinja]][[ja:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226516]] Wikidata name: Kagami-no-miya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226516]] Wikidata name: Kagami-no-miya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.48722|N|136.75222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|matsusha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ([[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-chō, Ise|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], it sits at the confluence of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by [[:en:Tanigawa|Tanigawa]] (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|qq=}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|sacred mirror]].<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]].<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Sengen shrine|lt=Sengen shrine|lt=Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]], with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from [[:en:Asama Station|Asama Station]]. ** From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama IC|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Line|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kagami-no-miya-jinja]][[ja:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226516]] Wikidata name: Kagami-no-miya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226516]] Wikidata name: Kagami-no-miya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.48722|N|136.75222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|matsusha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ([[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-chō, Ise|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], it sits at the confluence of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by [[:en:Tanigawa|Tanigawa]] (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|qq=}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|sacred mirror]].<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]].<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Sengen shrine|lt=Sengen shrine|lt=Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]], with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from [[:en:Asama Station|Asama Station]]. ** From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama IC|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Line|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}.

It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-chō, Ise|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], it sits at the confluence of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by [[:en:Tanigawa|Tanigawa]] (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|qq=}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|sacred mirror]].<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]].<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Sengen shrine|lt=Sengen shrine|lt=Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]], with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from [[:en:Asama Station|Asama Station]]. ** From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama IC|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Line|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}.

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-chō, Ise|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], it sits at the confluence of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by [[:en:Tanigawa|Tanigawa]] (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|qq=}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|sacred mirror]].<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]].<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Sengen shrine|lt=Sengen shrine|lt=Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]], with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from [[:en:Asama Station|Asama Station]]. ** From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama IC|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Line|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kagami-no-miya-jinja]][[ja:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226516]] Wikidata name: Kagami-no-miya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226516]] Wikidata name: Kagami-no-miya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.48722|N|136.75222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|matsusha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ([[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-chō, Ise|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], it sits at the confluence of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by [[:en:Tanigawa|Tanigawa]] (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|qq=}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|sacred mirror]].<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]].<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Sengen shrine|lt=Sengen shrine|lt=Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]], with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from [[:en:Asama Station|Asama Station]]. ** From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama IC|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Line|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}.

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kagami-no-miya-jinja]][[ja:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kagami-no-miya-jinja]][[ja:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226516]] Wikidata name: Kagami-no-miya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226516]] Wikidata name: Kagami-no-miya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.48722|N|136.75222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|matsusha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ([[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-chō, Ise|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], it sits at the confluence of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by [[:en:Tanigawa|Tanigawa]] (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|qq=}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|sacred mirror]].<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]].<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Sengen shrine|lt=Sengen shrine|lt=Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]], with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from [[:en:Asama Station|Asama Station]]. ** From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama IC|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Line|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kagami-no-miya-jinja]][[ja:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kagami-no-miya-jinja]][[ja:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kagami-no-miya-jinja]][[ja:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kagami-no-miya-jinja]][[ja:鏡宮神社 (伊勢市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226516]] Wikidata name: Kagami-no-miya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17226516]] Wikidata name: Kagami-no-miya-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 鏡宮神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Kagami-no-miya-jinja. 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]朝熊町字西沖2266 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 45 | 経度秒 = 7.9 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 岩上二面神鏡霊 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}末社 |創建= |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.48722|N|136.75222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Kagamimiya Shrine''' (鏡宮神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|matsusha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ([[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]) (Inner Shrine). It holds the 16th position among the 16 {{ill|matsusha|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsusha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}} of the Inner Shrine<ref>Compiled by {{ill|Ujiyamada City Office|ja|宇治山田市役所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1929): pp. 13–14</ref>.
Located in {{ill|Asama-chō, Ise|ja|朝熊町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-chō, Ise|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], it sits at the confluence of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]] and its tributary, the {{ill|Asama River|ja|朝熊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. On the opposite bank of the Asama River are {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Asakuma Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|lt=Asakuma Gozen Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakuma Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="nk5">Edited by [[:en:Tanigawa|Tanigawa]] (1986): p. 45</ref>.
== Overview == {{ill|Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|ja|鏡宮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami-no-Miya Shrine|qq=}} is a shrine dedicated to enshrining the [[:en:Mirrors in Shinto|sacred mirror]].<ref name="ic">Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.107</ref> The enshrined deity is "Iwa-no-Ue no Futatsu no Mikagami no Mitama" (岩上二面神鏡霊), with the mirror serving as its [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]].<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.60</ref>
The shrine name "Kagami-no-Miya" was originally one of the alternate names for {{ill|Sengen shrine|lt=Sengen shrine|lt=Asama Shrine|ja|朝熊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="nk1">Tanigawa, Ed. (1986): p.51</ref> The name derives from the enshrinement of two mirrors, one white and one bronze, at Asama Shrine. In 1663 (Kanbun 3), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was re-established as the front shrine (Gomae-sha) of Asama Shrine.<ref name="nk1"/> Although Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine, and Kagami-no-Miya Shrine are less than 100 meters apart in a straight line, crossing the Asama River via the public bridge required a lengthy detour. To facilitate rituals, a narrow pedestrian bridge was constructed, reducing the distance traveled to about 200 meters.<ref>Ise Cultural Publishing (2008): p.105</ref>
== Precincts == The shrine precincts cover approximately 803 square meters (8 are 3 bu).<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office, Ed. (1929): p.14</ref> The grounds feature the "Toraishi" (虎石), surrounded by a wooden fence, and the "Shiohiiwa" (潮干石) located in the river, along with numerous other rocks.<ref name="ic"/> Many of the trees in the precinct are pine trees.<ref name="ic"/>
The "Toraishi" was previously used as a [[:en:Yorishiro|yorishiro]], with two mirrors enshrined atop it.<ref name="gk"/>
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. It is not mentioned in the "Kōtaijingū Gishikichō" (皇太神宮儀式帳).<ref name="st">Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> After being rebuilt in 1663 (Kanbun 3) as the front shrine of Asama Shrine, Asama Gomae Shrine was later constructed next to Asama Shrine, establishing Kagami-no-Miya Shrine as an independent shrine.<ref name="nk1"/>
In June 1872 (Meiji 5), the Ministry of Religion removed shrines not listed in ancient documents from the jurisdiction of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], transferring control to Watari Prefecture and Mie Prefecture, thus separating Kagami-no-Miya Shrine from Ise Shrine's oversight.<ref name="st"/> However, Ise Shrine officials argued that, although not mentioned in the Gishikichō, the shrine's existence was confirmed in other ancient texts and it held special reverence.<ref name="st"/> This argument was accepted, and in 1876 (Meiji 9), Kagami-no-Miya Shrine was reinstated as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine.<ref name="st"/>
Subsequently, a debate arose within Ise Shrine about whether Kagami-no-Miya Shrine should be classified as a subordinate shrine of Asama Shrine due to their close ties.<ref name="st"/> Ultimately, Kagami-no-Miya Shrine remained as a subordinate shrine of the Inner Shrine without transferring under Asama Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.272-273</ref>
== Access == ; Public Transport * {{ill|Kintetsu Toba Line|ja|Toba Line|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Toba Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135505724}} ** Approximately 18 minutes on foot (about 1.5 km) from [[:en:Asama Station|Asama Station]]. ** From [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for "Meotoiwa Higashiguchi," alight at "Asama Interchange" bus stop, then walk approximately 5 minutes (about 470 m). Note: Bus services are infrequent, often suitable for either the outbound or return trip only. * Approximately 28 minutes on foot (about 2.3 km) from [[:en:Isuzugaoka Station|Isuzugaoka Station]] on the JR Sangu Line.
; By Car * About 3 minutes (about 1 km) from {{ill|Asama IC|ja|朝熊インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama IC|1=Asama interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092563}} on the {{ill|Ise Futami Toba Line|ja|伊勢二見鳥羽ライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Futami Toba Line|1=Ise Futami Toba Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378935}}.
